title: Part 1 – Interview (C1 Cambridge)
description: Evaluate an answer to the examiner's personal questions in Part 1 of Speaking C1 Advanced.
prompt: Acts as an official examiner for the Cambridge C1 Advanced (CAE). Evaluate my answer to Part 1 (Interview) according to the official Cambridge criteria: Grammar and Vocabulary, Discourse Management, Pronunciation and Interactive Communication. Rate each criterion from 1 to 5. Identify the fillers I have used and suggest alternatives C1. Next, give me 5 C1 level expressions that fit what I said. Here is my answer: [PASTE YOUR TEXT]

criteria.name: Grammar and Vocabulary
criteria.description: Use of varied and precise grammatical structures, together with sophisticated vocabulary appropriate for the C1 level.

criteria.name: Discourse Management
criteria.description: Ability to produce coherent, fluid and well-organized responses, using appropriate connectors and discourse markers.

criteria.name: Pronunciation
criteria.description: Speech clarity, fluency, and intelligibility, including appropriate intonation, accent, and rhythm.

criteria.name: Interactive Communication
criteria.description: Ability to initiate, maintain and develop interaction by responding in a natural and relevant way.

tips: Expand your answers with examples and justifications: 'For instance...', 'This is particularly evident when...'
Use advanced conditional structures: 'Were I to choose...', 'Had I known...'
Avoid monosyllabic responses. Always develop your ideas with at least 4-5 sentences.
Demonstrate lexical range with sophisticated synonyms: instead of 'important', use 'paramount', 'pivotal', 'indispensable'.
Add nuances with adverbs of degree: 'remarkably', 'arguably', 'undeniably', 'to a considerable extent'

speakingFeatures.examinerRole: Asks questions about the candidate's background, interests, opinions, and future plans
